title: pittosporum anomalum
intro: Pittosporum anomalum, also known as the 'Toi' or 'Kawa Kawa', is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly in its bark and seeds, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety, insomnia, and pain, as well as acting as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, Pittosporum anomalum is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal and therapeutic effects.
